title: rhinotropis minutifolia
intro: Rhinotropis minutifolia, also known as Dwarf St. Mary's spikenard, is a perennial herb native to the prairies of North America. It is a member of the Oenothera genus, a group of flowering plants that are often used in herbal medicine. Rhinotropis minutifolia has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it effective in treating wounds and skin conditions. The plant can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is said to offer health benefits such as reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and supporting immune function.
